seo.title: Building SaaS billing and payment integration with Stripe...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for SaaS billing and payment integration with Stripe and LemonSqueezy.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to building core SaaS functionality with focus on billing, authentication, and compliance. Follow these steps to implement critical components with production-grade considerations.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of PostgreSQL schema design

steps.title: Set up authentication and tenant identification
steps.explanation: Configure Clerk for user authentication and create a tenant identifier schema. Use Supabase's row-level security to isolate data between tenants.

code.content: ALTER TABLE tenants ADD COLUMN tenant_id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(); GRANT SELECT ON tenants TO anon; CREATE POLICY tenant_policy ON tenants FOR ALL USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant')::UUID);

pitfalls: Forgetting to set app.current_tenant in database connections
Not validating tenant_id in API routes

steps.title: Implement subscription billing with Stripe
steps.explanation: Create products and pricing tiers in Stripe Dashboard. Set up webhooks to handle subscription events and update tenant status in your database.

code.content: const stripe = require('stripe')('sk_test_...'); stripe.webhooks.on('checkout.session.completed', async (event) => { const session = event.data.object; const tenant = await getTenantByCustomerId(session.customer); await updateSubscriptionStatus(tenant, session); });

pitfalls: Not verifying webhook signatures with Stripe's endpoint secret
Ignoring trial periods in subscription logic

steps.title: Configure multi-tenancy database schema
steps.explanation: Design a shared database schema with tenant-specific data partitions. Use Supabase's built-in row-level security to enforce data isolation.

code.content: CREATE TABLE tenant_features (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id), feature_type TEXT NOT NULL, enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true); CREATE INDEX idx_tenant_features_tenant ON tenant_features(tenant_id);

pitfalls: Not indexing tenant_id columns in multi-tenant tables
Using global UUIDs instead of tenant-specific identifiers

steps.title: Add AI API cost tracking
steps.explanation: Instrument API calls to track usage against tenant quotas. Implement rate limiting and cost alerts using a time-series database.

code.content: from prometheus_client import Counter ai_api_calls = Counter('ai_api_calls_total', 'Total AI API calls per tenant', ['tenant_id', 'model']) def track_call(tenant_id, model): ai_api_calls.labels(tenant_id=tenant_id, model=model).inc()

pitfalls: Not sampling API usage data for cost estimation

steps.title: Implement GDPR data management
steps.explanation: Create endpoints for data export and deletion. Use Supabase's built-in retention policies to automatically purge inactive tenant data.

code.content: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION delete_tenant_data(tenant_id UUID) RETURNS void AS $$ BEGIN DELETE FROM user_activity WHERE tenant_id = tenant_id; DELETE FROM api_logs WHERE tenant_id = tenant_id; END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

pitfalls: Not anonymizing user data before deletion
Forgetting to update audit logs when data is removed

content.conclusion: This implementation sequence addresses core SaaS requirements with production considerations. Verify each component with integration tests and monitor metrics through PostHog for user behavior insights.
